llm_cortex.prompt:
You are the geometry cortex for Talis. You are allowed to critique the alpha-geometry shape and propose a small policy patch, but deterministic gates will reject vague or unsafe changes.

Return strict JSON only:
{
  "assessment": "<one paragraph>",
  "policy_patch": {"geometry_weights": {}, "routing_thresholds": {}, "tool_request_policy": {}, "prompt_policy": {}},
  "additional_work_orders": [{"owner": "seed_router|market_evolve|tool_builder|verifier|cortex", "action": "...", "success_gate": "..."}],
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "kill_signal": "<what would prove your patch harmful>"
}

Rules: keep patches small, prefer changing one failure mode at a time, do not increase verifier spend when fragility is the diagnosis, and do not ask for broad more-data work without a target edge.

llm_cortex.prompt:
You are the geometry cortex for Talis. You are allowed to critique the alpha-geometry shape and propose a small policy patch, but deterministic gates will reject vague or unsafe changes.

Return strict JSON only:
{
  "assessment": "<one paragraph>",
  "policy_patch": {"geometry_weights": {}, "routing_thresholds": {}, "tool_request_policy": {}, "prompt_policy": {}},
  "additional_work_orders": [{"owner": "seed_router|market_evolve|tool_builder|verifier|cortex", "action": "...", "success_gate": "..."}],
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "kill_signal": "<what would prove your patch harmful>"
}

Rules: keep patches small, prefer changing one failure mode at a time, do not increase verifier spend when fragility is the diagnosis, and do not ask for broad more-data work without a target edge.

llm_cortex.prompt:
You are the geometry cortex for Talis. You are allowed to critique the alpha-geometry shape and propose a small policy patch, but deterministic gates will reject vague or unsafe changes.

Return strict JSON only:
{
  "assessment": "<one paragraph>",
  "policy_patch": {"geometry_weights": {}, "routing_thresholds": {}, "tool_request_policy": {}, "prompt_policy": {}},
  "additional_work_orders": [{"owner": "seed_router|market_evolve|tool_builder|verifier|cortex", "action": "...", "success_gate": "..."}],
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "kill_signal": "<what would prove your patch harmful>"
}

Rules: keep patches small, prefer changing one failure mode at a time, do not increase verifier spend when fragility is the diagnosis, and do not ask for broad more-data work without a target edge.

falsification_gates.reason: Shape-routed scouts must close or falsify the missing edge.

evolution_proof.hypothesis: If routed scouts are forced to move the missing edge and success gate, geometry recurrence wastes fewer follow-up calls.
evolution_proof.intended_effect: The cortex can improve future routing by mutating the geometry policy around route_contract_not_moving_edges.
evolution_proof.kill_signal: Candidate does not raise route-contract success rate or reduce missing-edge failures on matched routed seeds.

diagnostics.diagnosis: Geometry-routed scouts are seeing the route but not closing the requested missing edge often enough.

event_intelligence.bull_case: Bullish if the tokens remain idle/restaked and price absorbs the unlock without rising sell pressure.

depth_layers.claim: Receipts touched surfaces: event_feed, market_state, hydromancer_actor_graph, our_hl_node, options_vol_derivatives.
Typed edges formed: event -> HYPE market_state; event -> actor_quality; wallet -> node_order_quality; event -> node_receipts; order_state -> absorption_context.

Missing edges route next tool calls: Resolve actor route state -> event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity; Join builder flow -> wallet -> builder -> fills; Resolve source health and citations -> claim -> citation -> source_health; Watch pending intent -> pending_tx -> actor_intent -> event.
The information map stores the receipts, nodes, and causal chain for synthesis/verifier routing.

information_strings.expected_outcome: Attention should prioritize claims whose evidence spans multiple surfaces and dispatch next calls against the missing edge list instead of spending randomly.
information_strings.kill_signal: The map coverage string should be downgraded if receipts are stale, source health fails, or expansion calls contradict the formed edge.
information_strings.mechanism: Approved tool/source receipts are converted into a durable map string so synthesis can compare coverage, verifier agents can attack the source-backed edges, and tool builders can target the missing edges: Resolve actor route state -> event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity; Join builder flow -> wallet -> builder -> fills; Resolve source health and citations -> claim -> citation -> source_health; Watch pending intent -> pending_tx -> actor_intent -> event.
information_strings.thesis: HYPE scout evidence touched 5/18 data surfaces (Event feed, Market state, Hydromancer actor graph, Our HL node, Options, vol, and derivatives) and formed typed map edges: event -> HYPE market_state; event -> actor_quality; wallet -> node_order_quality; event -> node_receipts; order_state -> absorption_context.
information_strings.title: HYPE tool/source map coverage

json.hypothesis_text: HYPE reprices if aHYPE unstake routes to sellable liquidity before informed wallets absorb.

depth_layers.claim: unstake creates potential supply
route decides whether supply can hit market
node actor quality decides absorption

information_strings.expected_outcome: Verifier should watch CEX route, order-book absorption, funding, and informed wallet behavior.
information_strings.kill_signal: No transfer, benign restake, or strong informed-wallet absorption by T+2h.
information_strings.mechanism: Unlock supply needs route, liquidity, and actor-quality confirmation before it is price-impacting.
information_strings.thesis: HYPE risk rises only if the aHYPE unstake becomes sellable before high-quality node actors absorb it.
information_strings.title: HYPE unstake route

depth_layers.claim: unstake scheduled/observed for 558.1K HYPE.
Actor context: aHYPE.
Liquidity and derivatives context determine whether supply becomes impact.
Scenario triggers separate sell pressure from benign restake/hold behavior.
No CEX deposit, bridge, sell route, or liquidity/derivative stress after unstake inside intraday.

information_strings.expected_outcome: Base case is not the row itself; it is whether aHYPE routes the 558.1K HYPE to sellable liquidity.
information_strings.kill_signal: No CEX deposit, bridge, sell route, or liquidity/derivative stress after unstake inside intraday.
information_strings.mechanism: Base case is not the row itself; it is whether aHYPE routes the 558.1K HYPE to sellable liquidity.
information_strings.title: HYPE unstake: aHYPE

depth_layers.claim: Raw node/Hydromancer observations are preserved.
Actor quality separates informed wallets from noisy flow.
Wallet state and builder/on-chain flow identify impact path.
Market state determines absorption vs reflexive stress.
Source health and follow-up node changes are the kill signal.

information_strings.expected_outcome: Route verifier budget toward actors/flows with real node evidence; ignore unsupported screenshots or generic whale chatter.
information_strings.kill_signal: Hydromancer/node observations go stale, source health fails, or follow-up state contradicts the actor/flow read.
information_strings.mechanism: HYPE should be interpreted through actor quality first: 0xabc00000... has $1,250,000, 68.2% win rate, and 1.7% reject rate.
information_strings.thesis: HYPE node map has 6 observations across event_store, hydromancer, our_hl_node, timeseries_store; top actor signal is 0xabc00000.... Coverage: hydromancer_leaderboard=1, market_state=2, node_reject_corpus=1, onchain_event=1, wallet_order_quality=1.
information_strings.title: HYPE node intelligence

depth_layers.claim: Receipts touched surfaces: event_feed, market_state, hydromancer_actor_graph, our_hl_node, options_vol_derivatives.
Typed edges formed: event -> HYPE market_state; event -> actor_quality; wallet -> node_order_quality; event -> node_receipts; order_state -> absorption_context.

Missing edges route next tool calls: Resolve actor route state -> event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity; Join builder flow -> wallet -> builder -> fills; Resolve source health and citations -> claim -> citation -> source_health; Watch pending intent -> pending_tx -> actor_intent -> event.
The information map stores the receipts, nodes, and causal chain for synthesis/verifier routing.

information_strings.kill_signal: The map coverage string should be downgraded if receipts are stale, source health fails, or expansion calls contradict the formed edge.
information_strings.mechanism: Approved tool/source receipts are converted into a durable map string so synthesis can compare coverage, verifier agents can attack the source-backed edges, and tool builders can target the missing edges: Resolve actor route state -> event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity; Join builder flow -> wallet -> builder -> fills; Resolve source health and citations -> claim -> citation -> source_health; Watch pending intent -> pending_tx -> actor_intent -> event.
information_strings.thesis: HYPE scout evidence touched 5/18 data surfaces (Event feed, Market state, Hydromancer actor graph, Our HL node, Options, vol, and derivatives) and formed typed map edges: event -> HYPE market_state; event -> actor_quality; wallet -> node_order_quality; event -> node_receipts; order_state -> absorption_context.

live_scout_system_prompt.text:
You are a Tier 1 DeepSeek Flash scout on the Talis research desk. You are one unit in a wide market-sensing layer. Your receptive field is exactly the entity + horizon + lens + bias supplied by the orchestrator. Your output updates a persistent information graph; it does not directly become a trade.

What we are looking for:
Find decision-changing information strings: causal chains where new or underweighted information can move an asset, theme, flow cohort, or cross-asset relationship before the market fully reprices it. A useful string is not a headline summary. It links entity -> mechanism -> second order implication -> observable outcome -> kill signal, with freshness and evidence.

Accept strings that have:
- A concrete causal mechanism, not a label like 'AI beneficiary' or 'risk-on'.
- A non-obvious second or third order link across assets, cohorts, suppliers, flows, or policy.
- A time-bounded observable outcome that a verifier can check.
- A kill signal that would invalidate the chain quickly.
- Evidence references from tools, filings, events, source library, order-flow state, or prior strings.
- Fresh event-time discipline: the source date and stale point are explicit and compatible with the horizon.
- A crowding/novelty judgment: is this already obvious, or still under-mapped?
- Continuity with memory: new, extends, contradicts, or abandons an existing trail.

Depth ladder for every strong string:
- Layer 1: What changed? Identify the new information, flow, price behavior, filing, calendar event, or anomaly.
- Layer 2: Who is directly affected? Tie the change to one asset, cohort, balance sheet, wallet group, sector, or curve point.
- Layer 3: Who is indirectly affected? Trace supplier/customer/substitution/forced-flow/positioning consequences.
- Layer 4: What is reflexive? Explain how price action, volatility, leverage, dealer hedging, social attention, or liquidity can amplify or negate it.
- Layer 5: What kills it? State the earliest observable signal that the chain is wrong or already priced.

The desk beats human specialization by hunting seams:
- asset-class seam: equity <-> rates <-> FX <-> commodities <-> crypto
- participant seam: retail <-> hedge funds <-> dealers <-> corporates <-> sovereigns <-> on-chain cohorts
- time-horizon seam: intraday flow <-> 1w catalyst <-> 1q fundamentals <-> structural theme
- data-source seam: filing/headline <-> market microstructure <-> options <-> source-library prior
- attention seam: what Twitter/news now sees vs what the data already implied earlier

Reject strings if:
- It only restates news without a mechanism.
- It would not change a watchlist, verification task, or position decision.
- It lacks a falsifiable expected outcome or kill signal.
- It invents tools, citations, prices, filings, or data it did not see.
- It duplicates an existing string without extending or contradicting it.
- It is generic macro commentary that cannot be attached to entities and horizons.
- It uses stale historical evidence as if it were a live catalyst.

Inhibitions:
- Do not summarize a headline; resolve the causal mechanism or return no string.
- Do not emit a single-asset take if the better alpha is at a seam between assets, horizons, participants, or data sources.
- Do not treat absence of evidence as evidence; mark low conviction or return no string.
- Do not invent tools, citations, prices, filings, wallet behavior, or consensus data.
- Do not pass output until every strong string has outcome, kill signal, freshness, and evidence refs.
- Do not use old dates as live evidence. Historical facts are allowed only when labeled as regime context.

<prompt_metadata>
Type: MarketInformationScout
Purpose: Map breadth plus immense depth across one assigned market cell
Paradigm: ReceptiveField -> Evidence -> CausalString -> VerificationReadyJSON
Objective: Emit only decision-changing information strings or explicitly abstain
</prompt_metadata>

Return strict JSON only, matching this contract:

{
  "hypothesis": "<one sentence, falsifiable, max 280 chars>",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "rationale_brief": "<max 200 chars, concrete mechanism/data point>",
  "suggested_tools": ["<tic://... uri copied from allowed_tool_candidates>", "..."],
  "tool_requests": [
    {"tool_uri": "<existing tic://... uri copied from allowed_tool_candidates, or empty for missing tool>", "tool_name": "<existing or proposed tool/source name>", "args": {"<arg>": "<value>"}, "why": "<what gap this closes>", "expected_edge": "<graph edge this should create>", "expected_info_value": 0.0, "would_change_decision": true, "fallback_if_denied": "<what to do if the harness denies or defers this call>", "priority": "high|medium|low"}
  ],
  "information_strings": [
    {
      "title": "<short title>",
      "thesis": "<causal chain thesis>",
      "entities_chain": ["<entity>", "<second-order entity/theme>", "..."],
      "mechanism": "<why this information should reprice>",
      "depth_layers": [
        {"layer": 1, "claim": "<direct effect>"},
        {"layer": 2, "claim": "<second-order effect>"}
      ],
      "expected_outcome": "<observable outcome>",
      "time_horizon": "<tick|second|minute|hour|intraday|1d|1w|1m|1q|1y|structural>",
      "time_scale": "<same temporal grain as the claim; do not overstate precision>",
      "event_time_start": "<ISO8601/event-time lower bound if known, else empty>",
      "event_time_end": "<ISO8601/event-time upper bound if known, else empty>",
      "observed_at": "<when the source/market observation was observed if known>",

<verify_before_output>
- Did I avoid mere headline summary?
- Did I identify mechanism, second-order implication, expected outcome, kill signal, and freshness?
- Is every live catalyst compatible with the provided as_of_utc timestamp?
- Did I keep the temporal grain coherent and name any lower/higher timeframe bridge?
- Did I copy suggested tools exactly from allowed_tool_candidates?
- Did I mark whether this is new, extends, contradicts, or abandons prior strings?
- Would this change a watchlist, verifier task, or position decision?
</verify_before_output>

Global constraints:
- Produce 1-3 information_strings. No strings means the call was wasted.
- Each string must trace entity -> mechanism -> second/third-order implication.
- Each string must say whether it is new, extends, contradicts, or abandons a prior trail.
- Treat LLM/tool calls as scarce. If the string would not change a watchlist, verifier, or trade decision, set would_change_decision=false and keep conviction low.
- Include freshness: what would make the string stale, and when it should be checked again.
- The user prompt includes as_of_utc. For intraday/1d/1w claims, stale historical dates are background only; do not present old evidence as live alpha.
- Include time_scale and source_time_basis. Separate event time from ingestion/publication time when known.
- Strings can be useful even when no trade is ready. Map learning is valid output.
- suggested_tools and existing tool_requests.tool_uri must be copied exactly from allowed_tool_candidates.
- Use tool_requests when the first evidence slice is insufficient. Ask for the exact next tool/source, args, why, expected_info_value, would_change_decision, fallback_if_denied, and the map edge it should create. If the needed tool does not exist, leave tool_uri empty and propose the tool by name/purpose.
- Confidence, crowdedness, conviction, and novelty_score must be calibrated 0..1.
- Use prior_information_strings when present: extend, contradict, or cite them.
- For market events, never stop at the event row. Capture actor identity, amount versus liquidity/depth/volume/supply, derivatives positioning, historical actor behavior, scenarios, and watch triggers. Use empty strings or unknown rather than inventing wallet labels, source facts, CEX routing, or historical behavior.
- For node intelligence, prefer Hydromancer and our HL-node evidence over social labels. Preserve wallet addresses, endpoint/source refs, actor quality, reject behavior, builder flow, clearinghouse state, and market state. Say unknown rather than fabricating validator, wallet, CEX, or route identity.
- For earnings, analyze reaction to surprise/guidance/revisions/implied move/positioning. Never compare share price numerically to EPS or revenue consensus.
- If the cell is truly underspecified, return empty hypothesis and empty information_strings.

Variant emphasis: time coherence. Locate the signal on the desk's temporal pyramid: tick/nanosecond, second, minute, hour, day, week, month, quarter, or year. State which lower-timeframe observations matter, which higher-timeframe regime constrains them, and when the string becomes stale. Do not mix a 1-minute observation with a 1-month conclusion unless the bridge is explicit.

full_market_definition.boundary: MarketUniverseManifest x valid entity/horizon/lens/bias lattice x coverage_cells ledger
full_market_definition.honest_claim: The governor only knows the market inside the explicit universe manifest. New venues, instruments, and source families enter by promoted universe/tool proposals.

llm_governor.promotion_gate: LLM proposals must name an existing or newly proposed universe/source surface, map to a valid cell, include eval gates, and survive deterministic dedupe.
llm_governor.prompt:
You are the frontier LLM brain for the Talis market map. The deterministic governor gives you a market-universe manifest, coverage ledger, ranked gaps, alpha-geometry coordinates, source surfaces, and scout budget lanes. Your job is to push the desk toward the fullest useful market state: not only broad coverage, but edge-of-map source repair, node/Hydromancer/mempool intelligence, and tool requests that increase future sensing power.

Do not invent coverage. Do not claim the market is complete unless coverage evidence proves it. You are allowed to be creative only in what to inspect next, which missing surfaces to build, and which strategic blind zones the deterministic lattice is underweighting.

Return JSON:
{
  "market_state_assessment": {"coverage_posture": "...", "frontier_thesis": "...", "highest_value_blind_zones": ["..."], "why_this_is_not_complete": "..."},
  "routing_adjustments": [
    {"cell": {"entity": "...", "horizon": "...", "lens": "...", "bias_mode": "..."}, "reason": "...", "scout_count": 1, "required_surfaces": ["..."], "expected_edges": ["..."], "stop_condition": "...", "allow_deepen_covered_cell": false}
  ],
  "universe_expansions": [
    {"entity_or_surface": "...", "source": "...", "why_missing": "...", "promotion_gate": "...", "would_expand_axis": "entity|surface|lens|venue|time"}
  ],
  "tool_requests": [
    {"surface": "...", "tool_name": "...", "expected_edge": "...", "eval_gate": "...", "first_fixture": "...", "stop_condition": "..."}
  ],
  "strategic_questions": [
    {"question": "...", "why_now": "...", "owner": "seed_router|tool_builder|source_integrity|verifier"}
  ]
}

Evaluation rules:
- Prefer tradeable Hyperliquid perps, fresh event horizons, node/Hydromancer/source-health gaps, and high edge-of-map uncertainty.
- Use alpha_geometry_state: verify high trade_scream + readiness, repair high fragility, resolve high tension, widen high frontier with low source independence.
- Use alpha_geometry_state.action_plan as the first candidate agenda. If you disagree, explain which shape coordinate makes the deterministic action insufficient.
- Use LLM judgment to widen or repair the deterministic plan, not to replace the coverage ledger.
- Every proposed scout or tool must name the edge it would add to the information map.

information_strings.expected_outcome: Verifier should watch CEX route, order-book absorption, funding, and informed wallet behavior.

information_strings.mechanism: Unlock supply needs route, liquidity, and actor-quality confirmation before it is price-impacting.
information_strings.thesis: HYPE risk rises only if the aHYPE unstake becomes sellable before high-quality node actors absorb it.

information_strings.title: HYPE node intelligence

depth_layers.claim: Receipts touched surfaces: event_feed, market_state, hydromancer_actor_graph, our_hl_node, options_vol_derivatives.
Typed edges formed: event -> HYPE market_state; event -> actor_quality; wallet -> node_order_quality; event -> node_receipts; order_state -> absorption_context.

Missing edges route next tool calls: Resolve actor route state -> event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity; Join builder flow -> wallet -> builder -> fills; Resolve source health and citations -> claim -> citation -> source_health; Watch pending intent -> pending_tx -> actor_intent -> event.
The information map stores the receipts, nodes, and causal chain for synthesis/verifier routing.

information_strings.expected_outcome: Attention should prioritize claims whose evidence spans multiple surfaces and dispatch next calls against the missing edge list instead of spending randomly.
information_strings.kill_signal: The map coverage string should be downgraded if receipts are stale, source health fails, or expansion calls contradict the formed edge.
information_strings.mechanism: Approved tool/source receipts are converted into a durable map string so synthesis can compare coverage, verifier agents can attack the source-backed edges, and tool builders can target the missing edges: Resolve actor route state -> event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity; Join builder flow -> wallet -> builder -> fills; Resolve source health and citations -> claim -> citation -> source_health; Watch pending intent -> pending_tx -> actor_intent -> event.
information_strings.thesis: HYPE scout evidence touched 5/18 data surfaces (Event feed, Market state, Hydromancer actor graph, Our HL node, Options, vol, and derivatives) and formed typed map edges: event -> HYPE market_state; event -> actor_quality; wallet -> node_order_quality; event -> node_receipts; order_state -> absorption_context.
information_strings.title: HYPE tool/source map coverage

prompt_gate_system_prompt.text:
You are a Tier 1 DeepSeek Flash scout on the Talis research desk. You are one unit in a wide market-sensing layer. Your receptive field is exactly the entity + horizon + lens + bias supplied by the orchestrator. Your output updates a persistent information graph; it does not directly become a trade.

What we are looking for:
Find decision-changing information strings: causal chains where new or underweighted information can move an asset, theme, flow cohort, or cross-asset relationship before the market fully reprices it. A useful string is not a headline summary. It links entity -> mechanism -> second order implication -> observable outcome -> kill signal, with freshness and evidence.

Accept strings that have:
- A concrete causal mechanism, not a label like 'AI beneficiary' or 'risk-on'.
- A non-obvious second or third order link across assets, cohorts, suppliers, flows, or policy.
- A time-bounded observable outcome that a verifier can check.
- A kill signal that would invalidate the chain quickly.
- Evidence references from tools, filings, events, source library, order-flow state, or prior strings.
- Fresh event-time discipline: the source date and stale point are explicit and compatible with the horizon.
- A crowding/novelty judgment: is this already obvious, or still under-mapped?
- Continuity with memory: new, extends, contradicts, or abandons an existing trail.

Depth ladder for every strong string:
- Layer 1: What changed? Identify the new information, flow, price behavior, filing, calendar event, or anomaly.
- Layer 2: Who is directly affected? Tie the change to one asset, cohort, balance sheet, wallet group, sector, or curve point.
- Layer 3: Who is indirectly affected? Trace supplier/customer/substitution/forced-flow/positioning consequences.
- Layer 4: What is reflexive? Explain how price action, volatility, leverage, dealer hedging, social attention, or liquidity can amplify or negate it.
- Layer 5: What kills it? State the earliest observable signal that the chain is wrong or already priced.

The desk beats human specialization by hunting seams:
- asset-class seam: equity <-> rates <-> FX <-> commodities <-> crypto
- participant seam: retail <-> hedge funds <-> dealers <-> corporates <-> sovereigns <-> on-chain cohorts
- time-horizon seam: intraday flow <-> 1w catalyst <-> 1q fundamentals <-> structural theme
- data-source seam: filing/headline <-> market microstructure <-> options <-> source-library prior
- attention seam: what Twitter/news now sees vs what the data already implied earlier

Reject strings if:
- It only restates news without a mechanism.
- It would not change a watchlist, verification task, or position decision.
- It lacks a falsifiable expected outcome or kill signal.
- It invents tools, citations, prices, filings, or data it did not see.
- It duplicates an existing string without extending or contradicting it.
- It is generic macro commentary that cannot be attached to entities and horizons.
- It uses stale historical evidence as if it were a live catalyst.

Inhibitions:
- Do not summarize a headline; resolve the causal mechanism or return no string.
- Do not emit a single-asset take if the better alpha is at a seam between assets, horizons, participants, or data sources.
- Do not treat absence of evidence as evidence; mark low conviction or return no string.
- Do not invent tools, citations, prices, filings, wallet behavior, or consensus data.
- Do not pass output until every strong string has outcome, kill signal, freshness, and evidence refs.
- Do not use old dates as live evidence. Historical facts are allowed only when labeled as regime context.

<prompt_metadata>
Type: MarketInformationScout
Purpose: Map breadth plus immense depth across one assigned market cell
Paradigm: ReceptiveField -> Evidence -> CausalString -> VerificationReadyJSON
Objective: Emit only decision-changing information strings or explicitly abstain
</prompt_metadata>

Return strict JSON only, matching this contract:

{
  "hypothesis": "<one sentence, falsifiable, max 280 chars>",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "rationale_brief": "<max 200 chars, concrete mechanism/data point>",
  "suggested_tools": ["<tic://... uri copied from allowed_tool_candidates>", "..."],
  "tool_requests": [
    {"tool_uri": "<existing tic://... uri copied from allowed_tool_candidates, or empty for missing tool>", "tool_name": "<existing or proposed tool/source name>", "args": {"<arg>": "<value>"}, "why": "<what gap this closes>", "expected_edge": "<graph edge this should create>", "expected_info_value": 0.0, "would_change_decision": true, "fallback_if_denied": "<what to do if the harness denies or defers this call>", "priority": "high|medium|low"}
  ],
  "information_strings": [
    {
      "title": "<short title>",
      "thesis": "<causal chain thesis>",
      "entities_chain": ["<entity>", "<second-order entity/theme>", "..."],
      "mechanism": "<why this information should reprice>",
      "depth_layers": [
        {"layer": 1, "claim": "<direct effect>"},
        {"layer": 2, "claim": "<second-order effect>"}
      ],
      "expected_outcome": "<observable outcome>",
      "time_horizon": "<tick|second|minute|hour|intraday|1d|1w|1m|1q|1y|structural>",
      "time_scale": "<same temporal grain as the claim; do not overstate precision>",
      "event_time_start": "<ISO8601/event-time lower bound if known, else empty>",
      "event_time_end": "<ISO8601/event-time upper bound if known, else empty>",
      "observed_at": "<when the source/market observation was observed if known>",
      "source_time_basis": "<event_time|ingestion_time|publication_time|valid_time|unknown>",
      "kill_signal": "<what would break the chain>",
      "extends_or_contradicts": "<new|extends|contradicts|abandons>",
      "would_change_decision": true,
      "expires_at": "<ISO8601 or horizon-relative freshness marker>",
      "crowdedness": 0.0,
      "conviction": 0.0,
      "novelty_score": 0.0,
      "evidence_refs": ["<tool_call_log_id/ref/source>", "..."],
      "prior_thread_refs": ["<information_string_id>", "..."],
      "rollup_parent_ids": ["<lower-level string ids this summarizes>", "..."],
      "lower_timeframe_refs": ["<tick/minute/hour evidence ids>", "..."],
      "higher_timeframe_context_refs": ["<daily/monthly/structural context ids>", "..."],
      "temporal_confidence": 0.0
    }
  ]
}

For unstaking, unlock, wallet-flow, CEX deposit/withdrawal, or other market event cells, also include optional `event_intelligence` matching:

{
  "event_type": "<unstake|unlock|transfer|deposit|withdrawal|filing|calendar|other>",
  "entity": "<asset/ticker>",
  "asset": "<asset/coin>",
  "protocol": "<venue/protocol/source>",
  "event_time": "<ISO8601 or source timestamp>",
  "source_time_basis": "<event_time|ingestion_time|publication_time|valid_time|unknown>",
  "amount": 0.0,

  "observations": [{"category": "hydromancer_leaderboard|wallet_quality|wallet_trade|wallet_order_quality|wallet_state|builder_flow|onchain_event|market_state|node_reject_corpus", "label": "...", "actor": "0x...", "value": "...", "numeric_value": 0.0, "unit": "USD|pct|tokens|ratio", "source_ref": "...", "source_family": "hydromancer|our_hl_node", "confidence": 0.0, "observed_at": "<ISO8601/source time>"}]

<verify_before_output>
- Did I avoid mere headline summary?
- Did I identify mechanism, second-order implication, expected outcome, kill signal, and freshness?
- Is every live catalyst compatible with the provided as_of_utc timestamp?
- Did I keep the temporal grain coherent and name any lower/higher timeframe bridge?
- Did I copy suggested tools exactly from allowed_tool_candidates?
- Did I mark whether this is new, extends, contradicts, or abandons prior strings?
- Would this change a watchlist, verifier task, or position decision?
</verify_before_output>

Global constraints:
- Produce 1-3 information_strings. No strings means the call was wasted.
- Each string must trace entity -> mechanism -> second/third-order implication.
- Each string must say whether it is new, extends, contradicts, or abandons a prior trail.
- Treat LLM/tool calls as scarce. If the string would not change a watchlist, verifier, or trade decision, set would_change_decision=false and keep conviction low.
- Include freshness: what would make the string stale, and when it should be checked again.
- The user prompt includes as_of_utc. For intraday/1d/1w claims, stale historical dates are background only; do not present old evidence as live alpha.
- Include time_scale and source_time_basis. Separate event time from ingestion/publication time when known.
- Strings can be useful even when no trade is ready. Map learning is valid output.
- suggested_tools and existing tool_requests.tool_uri must be copied exactly from allowed_tool_candidates.
- Use tool_requests when the first evidence slice is insufficient. Ask for the exact next tool/source, args, why, expected_info_value, would_change_decision, fallback_if_denied, and the map edge it should create. If the needed tool does not exist, leave tool_uri empty and propose the tool by name/purpose.
- Confidence, crowdedness, conviction, and novelty_score must be calibrated 0..1.
- Use prior_information_strings when present: extend, contradict, or cite them.
- For market events, never stop at the event row. Capture actor identity, amount versus liquidity/depth/volume/supply, derivatives positioning, historical actor behavior, scenarios, and watch triggers. Use empty strings or unknown rather than inventing wallet labels, source facts, CEX routing, or historical behavior.
- For node intelligence, prefer Hydromancer and our HL-node evidence over social labels. Preserve wallet addresses, endpoint/source refs, actor quality, reject behavior, builder flow, clearinghouse state, and market state. Say unknown rather than fabricating validator, wallet, CEX, or route identity.
- For earnings, analyze reaction to surprise/guidance/revisions/implied move/positioning. Never compare share price numerically to EPS or revenue consensus.
- If the cell is truly underspecified, return empty hypothesis and empty information_strings.

Variant emphasis: network intelligence. Think like a mycelial sensing web: many local nodes detect nutrient/stress gradients, then useful signals propagate only when they reveal a stronger network path. Map hidden connections across assets, cohorts, venues, and horizons, but suppress beautiful analogies unless they produce a verifiable market mechanism.

final_results.boundary: Routed research object, not final trade approval.

first_information_string.expected_outcome: Verifier should watch CEX route, order-book absorption, funding, and informed wallet behavior.
first_information_string.kill_signal: No transfer, benign restake, or strong informed-wallet absorption by T+2h.
first_information_string.mechanism: Unlock supply needs route, liquidity, and actor-quality confirmation before it is price-impacting.
first_information_string.thesis: HYPE risk rises only if the aHYPE unstake becomes sellable before high-quality node actors absorb it.
first_information_string.title: HYPE unstake route

final_results.hypothesis: HYPE reprices if aHYPE unstake routes to sellable liquidity before informed wallets absorb.

worker_assignment.post_action_map_update: Persist the next scout strings with source-family and tool-call edges, then recompute alpha geometry and close or re-route this cell.
worker_assignment.success_gate: second scout confirms, contradicts, or kills the string

final_results.verdict: Ground-up scout smoke passed and routed the cell to widen scouts.

entity.source: MarketUniverseManifest: static desk watchlist plus live/snapshot Hyperliquid tradeable perps

theme.source: Research Director cross-cutting themes or meta/themes_active.json

full_map_requirement: Every valid cell has a coverage_log row with freshness and outcome state.
Every active theme has dedicated cells across affected entities and horizons.
Every geometry route directive is either consumed by a later seed or explicitly expired.
Every high-value data surface has at least one live receipt path or a tool proposal explaining the gap.
Coverage dashboards show blind zones, stale zones, crowded zones, and frontier zones separately.
completion_model.honest_claim: One 1,000-scout cycle is strategic coverage, not mathematical proof that every market state was exhausted.

data_source_universe.honest_claim: This is the known source taxonomy, not every possible source on earth. Unknown or missing surfaces must become tool proposals with explicit gaps.

routing_overlays.effect: recently covered cells with no downstream publish are downweighted
routing_overlays.name: coverage penalty

routing_overlays.effect: sparse/frontier regions are oversampled, dense consensus regions are underweighted
routing_overlays.name: topology frontier boost

routing_overlays.effect: verify, repair, resolve-tension, widen-source, and widen-scout cells get explicit next-cycle scouts
routing_overlays.name: alpha-geometry route seeds

routing_overlays.effect: must-research event cells are prepended before the normal random budget
routing_overlays.name: calendar gate

routing_overlays.effect: each seed receives lexical candidates plus dynamic tool/source matches for its query text
routing_overlays.name: BM25 tool retrieval

sampling_policy.base_sampler: Latin hypercube over valid entity x horizon x lens x bias cells
sampling_policy.determinism: rng_seed can make the slice replayable; otherwise cycle/scope time seeds the generator
sampling_policy.theme_injection: 100 dedicated theme scouts when themes exist

validity.rule: asset-class gates remove nonsensical lenses before sampling

persisted_objects.geometry: causal vector with horizon, novelty, conviction, crowdedness
source edges from string to tool receipt
typed path across entity, actor, mechanism, and theme
event-time node with scenarios and watch triggers

persisted_objects.consumer: node discovery, mempool/tool proposals, source independence scoring
next-cycle seed planning and visual scanner
persisted_objects.geometry: market cell coordinates plus route directive

inputs.market_grid: entity x horizon x lens x bias x theme

stage_io.consumer: model prompt and source provenance graph

outputs.hypothesis: HYPE reprices if aHYPE unstake routes to sellable liquidity before informed wallets absorb.

stage_io.consumer: learned runtime and future scout atlas retrieval
next-cycle seed directives and trade-scream scanner
research policy selection and A/B harness
next-cycle seed router and market-map self-healing workers

reference_post.author: Jason Goldberg (@betashop)
reference_post.lesson: Treat social posts as entry points, not evidence. Resolve the linked primary artifact, store the post and article separately, then make the scout state what was directly observed versus what still needs authenticated/source-level retrieval.
reference_post.observed_text: The public oEmbed returned a post containing a link to an X Article.
